name: UART Bus
description: UART is a common serial protocol for many devices. For example, when uploading a binary to your ESP you have probably used UART to access the chip. UART (or for Arduino often also called Serial) usually consists of 2 pins:

config_entries.label: Baud Rate
config_entries.description: The baud rate of the UART bus.

config_entries.label: Debug
config_entries.description: Log UART traffic to the ESPHome log for troubleshooting. Bare `debug:` enables hex logging with sensible defaults.

config_entries.label: Direction
config_entries.description: Which side of the bus to log. Defaults to `BOTH`.

config_entries.label: Debug Prefix
config_entries.description: Prefix prepended to every debug log line. Useful when multiple UART buses log at the same time.

config_entries.label: Dummy Receiver
config_entries.description: Capture incoming bytes even when no UART device component is bound to the bus. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: After
config_entries.description: When to flush accumulated bytes to the log.

config_entries.label: Bytes
config_entries.description: Flush after this many bytes have been accumulated. Defaults to 150.

config_entries.label: Timeout
config_entries.description: Flush after no bytes have been seen for this long. Defaults to `100ms`.

config_entries.label: Delimiter
config_entries.description: Flush as soon as this byte sequence is seen in the stream.

config_entries.label: Flow Control Pin
config_entries.description: ESP32 only. The pin used to for hardware RS485 flow control. Use of this setting enables half-duplex mode. Use the full pin schema and set `inverted: true` to invert logic levels.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Port
config_entries.description:
**Required — set at least one of:** `tx_pin`, `rx_pin`, `port`.

Host platform only. Unix style name of the port to use.

config_entries.label: RX Pin
config_entries.description:
**Required — set at least one of:** `tx_pin`, `rx_pin`, `port`.

The pin to receive data on from the ESP's perspective. Use the full pin schema and set `inverted: true` to invert logic levels. Not supported by host platform.

config_entries.label: TX Pin
config_entries.description:
**Required — set at least one of:** `tx_pin`, `rx_pin`, `port`.

The pin to send data to from the ESP's perspective. Use the full pin schema and set `inverted: true` to invert logic levels. Not supported by host platform.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for this UART hub if you need multiple UART hubs.

config_entries.label: Data Bits
config_entries.description: The number of data bits used on the UART bus. Options: 5 to 8. Defaults to 8.

config_entries.label: Flush Timeout
config_entries.description: ESP32 only. The maximum time to wait for the TX FIFO to drain when `flush()` is called. If the TX FIFO does not drain within this time, `flush()` returns a `TIMEOUT` result. When not set, `flush()` waits indefinitely.

config_entries.label: Parity
config_entries.description: The parity used on the UART bus. Options: `NONE`, `EVEN`, `ODD`. Defaults to `NONE`.

config_entries.label: RX Buffer Size
config_entries.description: The size of the buffer used for receiving UART messages. Increase if you use an integration that needs to read big payloads from UART. Defaults to `256`.

config_entries.label: RX Full Threshold
config_entries.description: ESP32 only. After receiving this number of bytes, the data becomes available for processing. The default is calculated at compilation time to be approximately ten milliseconds (about 8 bytes at 9600 baud, 114 bytes at 115200 baud).

config_entries.label: RX Timeout
config_entries.description: ESP32 only. This value specifies a number of bytes used to determine the duration of the timeout. The duration of the timeout is based on the amount of time it would take to receive this number of bytes. In other words, for a given value and baud rate, doubling the baud rate will halve the duration of the timeout. After the timeout has elapsed, the data becomes available for processing. Defaults to `2`.

config_entries.label: Stop Bits
config_entries.description: The number of stop bits to send. Options: 1, 2. Defaults to 1.
